feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Cavaliers' Kenny Atkinson drops strong ex-Laker LeBron James hint amid free agency rumors
entry.content: The Cleveland Cavaliers are doing whatever it takes to reunite with LeBron James. Head coach Kenny Atkinson just gave major insight into their offseason plans.

entry.title: LeBron James emerges as Lakers' biggest threat in pursuit of Warriors NBA champion Jonathan Kuminga
entry.content: The Los Angeles Lakers are in hot pursuit of Jonathan Kuminga. Even though LeBron James is gone, he might still find a way to get back at the Lakers for their fading relationship.

entry.title: Lakers' $66M promising duo makes shocking Summer League decision amid trade rumors
entry.content: The Los Angeles Lakers have had some guys on the trade block for some time. However, with the appearances at Summer League, the vibe seems to be odd.

entry.title: Alex Caruso sends a clear message on Thunder’s strategy at the American Century Championship
entry.content: Alex Caruso believes in what the Oklahoma City Thunder have built and told me so at the 2026 American Century Championship.

entry.title: What time is the Home Run Derby tonight? TV channel, schedule and more to watch 2026 MLB All-Star event
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about watching the Home Run Derby, including the full schedule and start times for all of All-Star Week.

entry.title: Lakers officially sign $185M fan favorite, Luka Doncic's partner in crime for third straight season
entry.content: The Los Angeles Lakers made one of their offseason signings official on Sunday. Luka Doncic's future with another LA superstar is a done deal.

entry.title: Mini-pitches bringing soccer to new U.S. neighborhoods
entry.content: With the World Cup boosting America's interest in the game, one organization is building hundreds of smaller soccer pitches across the country. Michael George shows how it's changed a New Jersey school in the shadow of MetLife Stadium, where the World Cup final will take place.

entry.title: Caleb Wilson thinks UNC making him not shoot threes helped him reach the Bulls
entry.content: Caleb Wilson has become a knockdown shooter after joining the Chicago Bulls.

entry.title: Museums catching World Cup fever
entry.content: As World Cup teams head into the final stretch of the tournament, museums around the country are seizing on the renewed interest in soccer. There are special exhibits that explain aspects of the game that might surprise you. Bradley Blackburn reports.

entry.title: Training World Cup fans on how to perform CPR
entry.content: As the World Cup continues, the American Heart Association has a big goal of its own: to save lives with simple CPR training. Bradley Blackburn reports.

entry.title: Brady Tkachuk reps Team USA at the American Century Championship in a unique way
entry.content: Brady Tkachuk wore his Olympic gold medal during the 2026 American Century Championship at the Edgewood Tahoe Resort.

entry.title: Read full episode transcripts of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" from 2026
entry.content: Browse all of our full episode transcripts from 2026 of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan."

entry.title: Full transcript of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 12, 2026
entry.content: On this "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" broadcast, Republican Sen. Tim Scott remembers Lindsey Graham, and Israeli Ambassador Michael Leiter and retired Gen. Frank McKenzie discuss the Iran war.

entry.title: Spokane man completes number of grappler pullups for Guinness World Record attempt
entry.content: Jul. 12—Most people might spend their birthday relaxing with their loved ones by lounging by the river with a beer in hand. Most don't decide to break fitness world records on their special day. Lance Pecht, a former Whitworth University basketball player, spent his 43rd birthday Sunday at Body Unlimited Fitness, attempting the Guinness World Record for the most grappler pullups in one minute. ...

entry.title: Arizona Cardinals training camp roster preview: OL Ka'ena De Cambra
entry.content: We will preview every player on the Cardinals roster leading up to training camp. This is about OL Ka'ena De Cambra.

entry.title: Spokane Indians drop series finale to Eugene 9-5; head into MLB All-Star break in first | NWL baseball
entry.content: Jul. 12—EUGENE — Eugene's Lisbel Diaz hit two two-run home runs and the Spokane Indians lost to the Emeralds 9-5 in the finale of a six-game High-A Northwest League series at PK Park on Sunday. Diaz finished 2-for-4 with four RBIs. The Indians (14-7) were led by Jacob Hinderleider, who had two hits, including a home run, and three RBIs. Indians outfielder Tommy Hopfe hit the first pitch of the ...

entry.title: Rays 2026 Draft: Rounds 11-20
entry.content: The back half of the MLB Draft is where we leave the bonus pool minimums behind. Some players might sign for pretty decent amounts, but it’s the wild west and most will not demand much compensation to fill the Rays org needs. Out of their 21 selections overall, the Rays took 17 pitchers, and that […]

entry.title: Husker Wrestling: Incoming Freshman Parrow Wins Fargo National Title in Freestyle
entry.content: Nebraska also had 2027 commit Mac Crosson in the final, while Mason Petersen took 6th place

entry.title: 58 million under heat alerts as extreme temperatures scorch western U.S.
entry.content: Heat alerts were issued for millions across parts of the western U.S. Sunday as an unusually prolonged heat dome reached its peak.

entry.title: Frank Vogel expected to join Golden State Warriors' coaching staff
entry.content: Former Los Angeles Lakers head coach Frank Vogel will reportedly join Steve Kerr's coaching staff in Golden State. Here's the latest.

entry.title: Mets' 2026 MLB Draft recap: New York replenishing farm system after 19 players selected
entry.content: The 2026 MLB Draft was a very important draft to help the Mets replenish a farm system that dropped to the No. 24 ranked system in baseball according to Baseball America.

entry.title: Top Arizona prospect signs with Texas A&M after going undrafted
entry.content: A&M strengthened its 2026 class as top Arizona prospect Ryan Harwood confirmed his commitment after the MLB Draft, adding elite power and athleticism.

entry.title: Extreme heat, wildfires and severe thunderstorms affecting millions across the U.S.
entry.content: More than 58 million Americans under weather alerts Sunday as wildfires burn in the west and severe thunderstorms impact large swaths of the country. Carter Evans reports and Andrew Kozak takes a look at the forecast.

entry.title: Justice Department Investigating Allegation Against UAW President Shawn Fain
entry.content: A federal monitor tasked with overseeing the union said Fain acted improperly to benefit his fiancée.

entry.title: World Cup down to final four teams
entry.content: The World Cup has reached its final week with just four teams remaining: France, Spain, England and Argentina. Shanelle Kaul reports.

entry.title: State lawmakers cry foul over new cap placed on film tax credits
entry.content: More than three dozen state legislators have sent a letter to Gov. Gavin Newsom and state government leaders warning of a threat to the film and TV tax credit program.

entry.title: California airport offers free Short Story Dispenser for travelers
entry.content: Travelers at Ontario Airport in California are getting a new way to kill time between flights. Joy Benedict reports.

entry.title: Protests grow in Houston after ICE officer kills Mexican man who'd been living in U.S. for 30 years
entry.content: Protests have been building in Houston after ICE officers killed a Mexican man who had lived in the U.S. more than 30 years. Local investigators and activists are still searching for video to explain what happened. Jason Allen reports.

entry.title: Inside Lindsey Graham’s Final Mission: Saving Ukraine
entry.content: The longtime South Carolina lawmaker returned home from an arduous overseas trip to relish a last moment of political triumph.

entry.title: The Quarter-Trillion-Dollar Onslaught of AI Bonds Is Testing Investors’ Limits
entry.content: Tech giants are borrowing even more than expected, weighing on bond prices.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Delight the world with compassion, kindness and grace.
Lucky Numbers: 49, 14, 92, 82, 98, 83

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: When heating leftovers, space out a circle in the middle of your food, it will heat up much more evenly.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Banging your head against a wall uses 150 calories an hour.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Carol Burnett
entry.content: “Words, once they are printed, have a life of their own.” — Carol Burnett

entry.title: McConnell says he ‘won’t be able to return to the Senate floor to vote quite yet’
entry.content: McConnell says he ‘won’t be able to return to the Senate floor to vote quite yet’

entry.title: Summer Games Done Quick once again raises over $2 million for Doctors Without Borders
entry.content: The annual speedrunning marathon event ran from July 5-11.

entry.title: Philadelphia area digs out from damage left by a severe series of microburst storms
entry.content: Residents of Philadelphia and some surrounding counties are cleaning up and assessing damage after a series of severe storms called microbursts swept through with winds up to 60 or 70 miles per hour

entry.title: Philadelphia area digs out from damage left by a severe series of microburst storms
entry.content: Residents in some Philadelphia neighborhoods and surrounding counties were cleaning up Sunday from widespread damage caused by a line of severe, short thunderstorms that passed through.

entry.title: What are your plans for AI Appreciation Day?
entry.content: The best way to celebrate AI Appreciation Day is to not.

entry.title: Pentagon releases new batch of UFO files, China's Tianwen-2 reaches its asteroid target, and more science stories
entry.content: Catch up on the most interesting science news from the last week.

entry.title: Lindsey Graham’s death will shake the Senate, and the November election. Here’s what comes next
entry.content: Lindsey Graham’s death will shake the Senate, and the November election. Here’s what comes next

entry.title: Changing conditions cause expanded evacuation orders for Ferris Fire
entry.content: Colorado officials expanded mandatory evacuation orders for residents near the Ferris Fire as conditions continued to change on Sunday.

entry.title: Archaeologists uncover 3,000-year-old tomb near Egypt's Luxor
entry.content: The burial site was identified as belonging to a man named Paser based on inscriptions.

entry.title: Xbox gamer wins lawsuit against Microsoft to restore account and digital library
entry.content: Microsoft also has to pay roughly $400 in damages to the user.

entry.title: Democratic Sen. Dick Durbin says Sen. Lindsey Graham was a "fair-minded person"
entry.content: Democratic Sen. Dick Durbin of Illinois joins CBS News 24/7 to discuss his friendship and political clashes with Sen. Lindsey Graham, who died suddenly this weekend.

entry.title: How Sen. Lindsey Graham's Senate seat will get filled
entry.content: Longtime South Caroline Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham died at 71 after a "brief and sudden illness," his office said early Sunday morning. CBS News' Major Garrett breaks down how his Senate seat will be filled.

entry.title: How Sen. Lindsey Graham's death impacts U.S. politics
entry.content: Sen. Lindsey Graham's sudden death has shocked Washington, D.C. Political strategist Kevin Sheridan joins CBS News 24/7 to break down Graham's impact on U.S. politics.

entry.title: Keystone Pipeline system's operator agrees to pay a $26.9M penalty over a major Kansas oil spill
entry.content: A proposed legal settlement with the U.S. government would require the Keystone Pipeline system's operator to pay a $26.9 million civil penalty over a major oil spill in Kansas in December 2022 and spend about $40 million more to prevent future accidents.

entry.title: Transcript: Rep. Mike Turner on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 12, 2026
entry.content: The following is the transcript of an interview with Republican Rep. Mike Turner of Ohio that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 12, 2026.

entry.title: Ukraine's prime minister resigns; Zelenskyy shifts several government roles
entry.content: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has stepped down as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced fresh changes to Ukraine's government.

entry.title: Refreshed Apple Pencils may arrive next year with improved repairability
entry.content: Thanks to upcoming EU regulations, the next Apple Pencil could feature replaceable batteries.

entry.title: Graham makes final remarks in Ukraine a day before this death
entry.content: Sen. Lindsey Graham met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and made public remarks during a trip to Kyiv just one day before his death. Graham said he was focused on advancing legislation aimed at imposing sanctions on Russian oil.

entry.title: Lindsey Graham, Influential GOP Senator, Dies at 71
entry.content: Plus, the scales tip in Ukraine, resistance to AI grows, and women hit “prime time” later in life.

entry.title: What is the process for filling Lindsey Graham's vacancy in the Senate?
entry.content: Sen. Lindsey Graham was running for reelection in November when he died suddenly on Saturday.

entry.title: Rahm Emanuel weighs in on the future of the U.S.-Israel relationship
entry.content: Former White House chief of staff Rahm Emanuel joined "Face the Nation" to discuss his "plan that gets out of Israel being a pariah." He expanded on his proposed "23-state solution" including Israel, the Palestinians and Arab states.

entry.title: Sen. Scott on Lindsey Graham's role in "building bridges," including with Trump
entry.content: Republican Sen. Tim Scott of South Carolina remembered Sen. Lindsey Graham as a "powerful leader" following his sudden death​, while emphasizing Graham's role in "building bridges."

entry.title: Transcript: Sen. Tim Scott on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 12, 2026
entry.content: The following is the transcript of an interview with Republican Sen. Tim Scott of South Carolina that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 12, 2026.

entry.title: Rep. Mike Turner hopes passing Russia sanctions bill will be part of Lindsey Graham's legacy
entry.content: Republican Rep. Mike Turner told "Face the Nation" that Senate passage of a Russia sanctions bill should be "one of the legacies" of Lindsey Graham, who pushed the bipartisan legislation shortly before his death Saturday.

entry.title: Why Xiaomi phones aren't banned, but are rarely sold in the US
entry.content: The Chinese company is allowed to sell its impressive phones in the States, but it doesn't. Here's why.

entry.title: What Dispatch Audio Tells Us About the Emergency Response at Lindsey Graham’s Home
entry.content: Firefighters responding at the senator’s Capitol Hill address got no answer. Radio traffic later described a man in cardiac arrest.

entry.title: Adam Schiff remembers Lindsey Graham as ‘larger than life’: Full interview
entry.content: Sen. Adam Schiff (D-Calif.) joins Meet the Press to reflect on the passing of his colleague Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) and discuss the U.S. war with Iran as both countries continue to trade strikes.

entry.title: Georgia teen charged in Apalachee High School shooting to appear in court for plea and sentencing
entry.content: A judge has set a plea and sentencing hearing later this month for a teenager accused of killing four people in a September 2024 shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia.

entry.title: Tim Scott remembers how Lindsey Graham ‘committed to the people he loved’: Full interview
entry.content: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Sen. Tim Scott (R-S.C.) reacts to the death of his fellow South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham (R).

entry.title: Netanyahu says he's lost a 'beloved friend' after the death of Lindsey Graham: Full interview
entry.content: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu remembers the late Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) for his “clarity… courage… and convictions.”

entry.title: The correct way to position your router's antennas depends on your home
entry.content: Know where to place your router and how to position its antennas should get you better coverage.

entry.title: Trump says ‘I have somebody that I like’ to succeed Lindsey Graham but ‘too soon’ to say who
entry.content: President Donald Trump joins Meet the Press in an exclusive interview and weighs in on the decision Gov. Henry McMaster (R-S.C.) now faces to appoint someone to fill the late Sen. Lindsey Graham’s (R-S.C.) seat. “Ultimately they'll have an election,” Trump said. “Some are good, some are bad. And but I have somebody that I like, but I'm not going to tell you who now because it's too soon.”

entry.title: U.S. and world leaders pay tribute to Lindsey Graham following sudden death
entry.content: The senior U.S. senator for South Carolina died suddenly at 71 on Saturday.

entry.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 12)
entry.content: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

entry.title: Almanac: July 12
entry.content: "Sunday Morning" looks back at historical events on this date.

entry.title: How white supremacists staged the only successful coup in U.S. history
entry.content: In 1898, Wilmington, N.C., was prosperous and integrated. But white supremacists took back control of the city's multi-racial government at gunpoint, and killed scores of Black residents - a little-known story retold in Lauren Collins' "They Stole a City."

entry.title: Trump remembers his last call with Lindsey Graham the night before his death: Full interview
entry.content: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Donald Trump reacts to the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.).

entry.title: How white supremacists staged the only successful coup in U.S. history
entry.content: In 1898, Wilmington, N.C., was a prosperous integrated city, where Black and white North Carolinians shared political power and leadership positions. But white supremacists took back control of the city's multi-racial government at gunpoint, and launched a wave of violence that killed scores of Black residents. That little-known history is the subject of The New Yorker journalist Lauren Collins' new book, "They Stole a City." She talks with Lee Cowan about her hometown's darkest chapter – the only successful coup in our nation's history.

entry.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies after "brief and sudden illness"
entry.content: President Trump paid tribute to the late senator, who was reportedly scheduled to do an interview on Sunday.

entry.title: US Launches New Strikes on Iran After It Closes Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: The United States launched new attacks on Iran after the Iranians, again, shut down the Strait of Hormuz. This comes hours after Iran’s new supreme leader vowed revenge for the death of his father in the opening hours of the war. NBC’s Raf Sanchez reports for Sunday TODAY.

entry.title: Goldman Sachs picks its favorite Chinese AI models
entry.content: The investment bank released its Chinese AI model competitive positioning framework on Friday.

entry.title: Special Report: Looking back on the political legacy of Sen. Lindsey Graham
entry.content: Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., who had held his Senate seat since 2003 and was seen as a war hawk and significant voice on defense and foreign policy, has died at the age of 71 from a “sudden illness.” NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin and Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker report on Graham’s rise in Congress, his relationship with President Donald Trump, and his views on the wars in Iran and between Russia and Ukraine.

entry.title: Nigel Farage Wanted a Political Showdown. Now He’s Fighting a Trash Can.
entry.content: Novelty candidates have long been a feature of British politics, but it’s rare for one to be the main competition.

entry.title: ‘I felt dizzy’: bodybuilder recalls how drug abuse caught up with him
entry.content: Jamie Mantzouridis used steroids, growth hormones and insulin until the side effects became impossible to ignoreFitness influencers linked to wellness brand helping run illegal steroid market on TelegramJamie Mantzouridis vividly remembers the first time he took steroids. He got someone else to inject him and his mind was racing. “What if he hit an artery? What if there was an air bubble?” Mantzouridis, however, says he was young and fearless.He was small growing up, skinny and self-conscious. By the time he started training seriously in his early 20s, he noticed other guys at the gym who were much larger, and they explained they were taking steroids. “They looked really good,” he says, “and they said that was what they were doing.” Continue reading...

entry.title: UK becoming ‘wild west’ for experimental peptides, expert warns
entry.content: Prof Channa Jayasena says growing online sales of unregulated drugs risk fatalities as responsibility falls between regulatorsFitness influencers linked to wellness brand helping run illegal steroid market on TelegramThe UK has become a “wild west” for people peddling experimental peptides, steroids and other substances, a leading expert has said, warning action must be taken to avoid fatalities.Prof Channa Jayasena of Imperial College London, a consultant in reproductive endocrinology and andrology at Hammersmith and St Mary’s hospitals, said he is now encountering patients “day in, day out” who are taking experimental peptides. Continue reading...

entry.title: Fitness influencers linked to wellness brand helping run illegal steroid market on Telegram
entry.content: Ambassadors for Gencore Global directed followers to Telegram channels promoting steroids, prescription medicines and experimental peptides‘I felt dizzy’: body builder recalls how drug abuse caught up with himUK becoming ‘wild west’ for experimental peptides, expert warnsFitness influencers who publicly represent a global wellness brand are involved in running an illegal steroid market on social media, the Guardian can reveal.Gencore Global presents itself as a UK-based health and wellness company and has recently appeared at FitXpo North West, a fitness event in Greater Manchester. It has also sponsored a racehorse, launched a UK combat sports and influencer boxing promotion, and is set to attend the National Running Show in Birmingham next year. Continue reading...

entry.title: MLB Futures Game rosters 2026: Full list of prospects playing includes Leo De Vries, Eli Willits
entry.content: MLB's next wave of talent will take the field in Philadelphia for the Futures Game. Here are the full rosters.

entry.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71 after ‘brief and sudden illness’
entry.content: Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., who was elected to the Senate in 2003 and was a close political ally of President Donald Trump, has died, his office confirmed early Sunday. He was 71.

entry.title: Switzerland manager criticises controversial new ‘mistaken identity’ rule after World Cup exit
entry.content: Switzerland’s first quarter-final in 72 years ended with Argentina instead booking a date with England in the last four

entry.title: Barcelona’s latest signing won’t impact pursuit of La Liga sensation – Romano
entry.content: Barcelona have moved quickly to secure a move for talented German attacker Karim Adeyemi from Borussia Dortmund.Much like the Anthony Gordon deal that they pulled off earlier this summer, there was li...

entry.title: Norway talisman heaps praise on Jude Bellingham after World Cup quarterfinal clash: ‘Real Madrid and England are lucky’
entry.content: Jude Bellingham is rewriting the history books with his FIFA World Cup campaign, and he is even a strong contender for the Golden Ball at this point in time.Taking to the field against Norway in Engla...

entry.title: This is ‘hell’ – Conor McGregor breaks silence on UFC 329 loss, claims no pre-fight injury
entry.content: Conor McGregor has issued his first statement since losing at UFC 329.

entry.title: World Cup QF Drama: Argentina Beats Swiss, England Overcomes Norway Cable Controversy & Semis Preview
entry.content: Christian Polanco and Alexis Guerreros go late-night on the ground in Miami to deliver a comprehensive live reaction to a wild day of World Cup quarterfinal action. The guys break down the regulatory madness of Breel Embolo's simulation red card in Argentina's tight 3-1 win over Switzerland, alongside the twin controversies of overturned goals and stadium skycam cable strikes during England's grueling 2-1 extra-time win over Norway. Evaluating standout masterclasses from Julián Álvarez and Jude Bellingham against an isolated, hobbling Erling Haaland, the hosts hand out final tournament grades to the exiting underdogs before dropping official score predictions for the locked-in Spain vs. France and England vs. Argentina semifinal blockbusters.

entry.title: U.S. conducts another round of retaliatory strikes on Iran, Pentagon says
entry.content: U.S. forces conducted their third round of strikes on Iran this week, this time in retaliation for an attack on a commercial ship in the Strait of Hormuz, the Pentagon said.

entry.title: Record breaker crosses Iceland in 'best-ever time'
entry.content: Jack Pitcher smashed the British record for travelling across Australia by foot in 2024.

entry.title: Whipsnakes bounce back with dominant offensive showing in win over Chaos
entry.content: The Whipsnakes had some soul-searching to do following their loss to the Denver Outlaws two weeks ago. Maryland’s offense was subpar, and its second-half execution meant it was handed a...

entry.title: Kansas woman convicted of double murder after three trials
entry.content: Mike Sisco and his girlfriend Karen Harkness were gunned down in her Topeka, Kansas, home in 2002. Authorities believed it was a crime of passion. Sisco's daughter set out to help prove it was her mother, Dana Chandler, who was responsible.

entry.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
entry.content: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

entry.title: Who is still in the World Cup? Updated list of teams left in 2026 FIFA semifinal bracket
entry.content: Here's a look at the four sides left in this year's competiton.

entry.title: UFC 329: Paddy Pimblett makes statement with quick submission victory
entry.content: While Conor McGregor took the headlines, Paddy Pimblett actually earned them at UFC 329.

entry.title: Where to watch MLB All-Star Futures Game: TV channel, live stream, time for baseball prospects showcase
entry.content: The Sporting News has all the details on how to watch the 2026 MLB All-Star Futures Game.

entry.title: My Mother's Murder Trials - Part 2
entry.content: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports for "48 Hours."

entry.title: Switzerland's well-earned reward for acting is ejection from game and elimination from World Cup
entry.content: Switzerland striker Breel Embolo delivered some convincing acting, but this wasn't a Broadway play. This was the World Cup. And with video review now so prominent in the sport, that gets you punished, not rewarded.

entry.title: Soccer world reacts to controversial red card that helped Argentina beat Switzerland, advance to World Cup semis
entry.content: Referee Joao Pinheiro's decision to send Breel Embolo off for "mistaken identity" left many onlookers feeling confused.

entry.title: Will Conor McGregor retire? Knee injury suffered vs, Max Holloway makes legend's future appear uncertain
entry.content: Here's the latest on whether or not McGregor will definitively close out his career with his latest injury.

entry.title: Social media reacts to Conor McGregor injuring knee in opening moments of return fight vs. Max Holloway
entry.content: Conor McGregor's attempt at a return ended as soon as the fight started. The Sporting News compiled reactions to the fight at UFC 329, highlighting discussions about McGregor's injury.
